title: Greek city states vs USA States -  Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle compares the ancient Greek city-state model to the American state system, arguing that power should be decentralized to the local level. He draws on Plato and Aristotle to support the idea that local governance gives people more freedom and investment in politics, and envisions a future where city-states and digital democracy coexist. He also discusses Boulder as his personal city-state project, YouTube culture, wealth inequality, and the Electoral College.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Prefers it; local autonomy gives people more freedom and political investment
Federal government is too top-heavy; more power should go to states and cities
Should be decided at the local/city level, not federally
Archaic and undemocratic; should be abolished in favor of direct popular vote
Worst form of government; Rome's autocratic period produced nothing worth reading
Should be reduced; Plato's 5:1 ratio is a good ideal; consumerist/influencer culture makes it worse
Homogeneity of values (not necessarily race) strengthens community cohesion
Technology could enable planet-scale direct democracy managed by computers, while preserving local community identity
Rich-lifestyle content is a harmful feedback loop analogous to the Dutch tulip bubble
